messages.content: Which tool would help you identify whether the correct permissions are in place for files or folders on a system you are monitoring? option A FIM option B NIDS option C NIPS option D None of these
Option A FIM (File Integrity Monitoring) would help you identify whether the correct permissions are in place for files or folders on a system you are monitoring.
What sort of guard can patrol an area 24/7/365 with very little downtime? option A Patrol cars option B Reception desk option C Armed guards option D Robot sentries
Option D Robot sentries can patrol an area 24/7/365 with very little downtime.
Which hard disk resilience mechanism uses two hard disks to provide redundancy and copies everything to both hard disks? option A RAID 6 option B RAID 10 option C RAID 0 option D RAID 3
Option B RAID 10 (also known as RAID 1+0) uses two hard disks to provide redundancy and copies everything to both hard disks.

Question 33 : What type of remote access is the most widely used secure method considering all your assets are located behind a firewall? option A VPN option B APN option C Private line terminal option D A rate-limited policy applied to inbound interfaces
Option A VPN (Virtual Private Network) is the most widely used secure method for remote access when all assets are located behind a firewall.

Which of the following terms means loading third-party apps from a location outside the official application store for that device? option A SIM card cloning option B Carrier unlocking option C Sideloading option D Jailbreaking
Option C Sideloading means loading third-party apps from a location outside the official application store for that device.

Which of the following is the first step when litigation has been filed or will soon be filed, with a focus on saving data in its current state? option A Data destruction option B Data collection option C Data recovery option D Data preservation
Option D Data preservation is the first step when litigation has been filed or will soon be filed, with a focus on saving data in its current state.
Not implementing a disaster recovery plan properly can lead to which of the following? option A Brand awareness option B Satisfied customers option C Faster recovery option D Lost revenue
Option D Lost revenue can occur when a disaster recovery plan is not implemented properly.

Which of the following are computer systems that run from resources stored on a central server or from the cloud instead of a local (on-premises) system? option A Thin clients option B Containers option C VPCs option D Edge devices
Option A Thin clients are computer systems that run from resources stored on a central server or from the cloud instead of a local (on-premises) system.

How does NetFlow create flow sessions from unidirectional sets of packets? option A NetFlow uses flows from stateless packets to build an aggregation of flow records. option B Unidirectional flows are sent to dynamic collectors. option C None of these. option D NetFlow statefully tracks flows or sessions, aggregating packets associated with each flow into flow records.
Option D NetFlow statefully tracks flows or sessions, aggregating packets associated with each flow into flow records. This allows NetFlow to create flow sessions from unidirectional sets of packets.
Which type of policy would define the rules that restrict how a computer may be used? option A All of these option B Privacy option C Acceptable use option D Credential
Option C Acceptable use policy would define the rules that restrict how a computer may be used.

_____________ risk assessment is an assessment that assigns numeric values to the probability of a risk and the impact it can have on the system or network. option A Quantitative option B None of these option C Measured option D Qualitative
Option A Quantitative risk assessment is an assessment that assigns numeric values to the probability of a risk and the impact it can have on the system or network.

_____________ risk assessment is an assessment that assigns numeric values to the probability of a risk and the impact it can have on the system or network. option A Quantitative option B None of these option C Measured option D Qualitative
Option A Quantitative risk assessment is an assessment that assigns numeric values to the probability of a risk and the impact it can have on the system or network.

Which of the following best describes the difference between smishing and vishing? option A  Vishing is a social engineering attack in which the attacker leaves a voicemail and then persuades the user to reveal sensitive information or perform a given action. Smishing is a type of typo squatting and pharming campaign using short Internet messaging systems. option B  None of these option C  Vishing is a social engineering attack in which the attacker calls the user over the phone and then persuades the user to reveal sensitive information or perform a given action. Smishing is a type of phishing campaign using SMS text messages instead of email. option D  Vishing is a social engineering attack in which the attacker leaves a voicemail and then persuades the user to reveal sensitive information or perform a given action. Smishing is a type of typo squatting and pharming campaign using Bluetooth.

Option C Vishing is a social engineering attack in which the attacker calls the user over the phone and then persuades the user to reveal sensitive information or perform a given action. Smishing is a type of phishing campaign using SMS text messages instead of email.

Which of the following usually entails not carrying out a proposed plan because the risk factor is too great? option A  Risk avoidance option B  Risk transference option C  Risk assessment option D  Risk mitigation
Option A  Risk avoidance usually entails not carrying out a proposed plan because the risk factor is too great.

Which of the following is a nonprofit organization established in 2008 with the goal of promoting security best practices in cloud computing environments? option A  NIST option B  CIS option C  CSA%C2%A0 option D  ISO
Option C CSA (Cloud Security Alliance) is a nonprofit organization established in 2008 with the goal of promoting security best practices in cloud computing environments.

Which of the following is a nonprofit organization established in 2008 with the goal of promoting security best practices in cloud computing environments? option A  NIST option B  CIS option C  CSA%C2%A0 option D  ISO
Option C CSA (Cloud Security Alliance) is a nonprofit organization established in 2008 with the goal of promoting security best practices in cloud computing environments.

Which term is used when you have a near duplicate of the original site of the organization that can be up and running within minutes? option A  Warm site option B  Cluster site option C  Cold site option D  Hot site
Option D Hot site is used when you have a near duplicate of the original site of the organization that can be up and running within minutes.
Which of these controls aim at monitoring and determining any unauthorized behavior? option A  Physical option B  Detective option C  Corrective option D  Managerial
Option B Detective controls aim at monitoring and determining any unauthorized behavior.
Which of these controls aim at monitoring and determining any unauthorized behavior? option A  Physical option B  Detective option C  Corrective option D  Managerial
Option B Detective controls aim at monitoring and determining any unauthorized behavior.

Which of the following describes when an attacker must first infect the victim’s computer with a trojan or a malicious browser extension or plug-in to intercept transactions from a web browser? option A On-path attack option B XSS attack option C SQL injection option D Keylogger injection
Option A On-path attack describes when an attacker must first infect the victim’s computer with a trojan or a malicious browser extension or plug-in to intercept transactions from a web browser.

______ refer(s) to the duration during which the statements written in any programming language are checked for mistakes. option A Unpatched applications and operating systems option B Misconfigured storage buckets option C Compile time option D Misconfigured VMs
Option C Compile time refers to the duration during which the statements written in any programming language are checked for mistakes.

Question 48 : Which process is required by GDPR to identify the risk of data compromise to an individual? option A  Terms of agreement option B  All of these option C  Impact assessment option D  Data control
Option C Impact assessment is required by GDPR to identify the risk of data compromise to an individual.

Lists are used in every manner of computer and application access. When you allow an application in your system, what type of list do you use? option A  Approved option B  Block option C  Allow option D  Deny
Option C Allow list is used when you allow an application in your system.
Question 50 : ___________ is the measured period of time between failures of a system. option A MTTF option B MTBR option C None of these option D MTBF
Option D MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) is the measured period of time between failures of a system.
Which tool can be used to validate who is logged in to a Windows system? option A whoami option B None of these option C Winlogon option D PSLoggedOn
Option D PSLoggedOn can be used to validate who is logged in to a Windows system.
Which email protocol allows a user to download all the messages and still retain a copy of the email on the email server? option A ADFS option B IMAP option C NMAP option D POP3
Option B IMAP (Internet Message Access Protocol) allows a user to download all the messages and still retain a copy of the email on the email server.

Which of the following is true about spear phishing attacks? option A  They%C2%A0are phishing attempts that are constructed in a very specific way and directly targeted to specific individuals or companies. option B  They%C2%A0use the Windows Administrative Center. option C  They%C2%A0use Windows PowerShell. option D  They are%C2%A0the%C2%A0same as%C2%A0whaling and phishing%C2%A0attacks.
Option A They are phishing attempts that are constructed in a very specific way and directly targeted to specific individuals or companies.

An attacker attempts to force an application to rollback the version of TLS (from TLS version 1.3 to 1.0). The given example is about _______. option A  cracking option B  fuzzing option C  privilege escalation option D  downgrade attack
Option D downgrade attack is when an attacker attempts to force an application to rollback the version of TLS (from TLS version 1.3 to 1.0).

Which of these is a structured way of modifying the state of a computer system, network, or IT procedure? option A None of these option B Asset management option C Change management option D Policy management
Option C Change management is a structured way of modifying the state of a computer system, network, or IT procedure.
Which of the following solutions would help you to secure your remote workers’ Internet access? option A CASB option B SWG option C IAM option D None of these
Option B SWG (Secure Web Gateway) solution would help you to secure your remote workers’ Internet access.

Question 57 : Which vulnerability can lead to stealing cookies or redirecting users to malicious sites and where the malicious code or script is permanently stored on a vulnerable system? option A Reflected XSS option B Stored XSS option C DOM-based XSS option D All of these

Option B Stored XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) vulnerability can lead to stealing cookies or redirecting users to malicious sites and where the malicious code or script is permanently stored on a vulnerable system.
Which type of malware can look like legitimate software but then performs negative actions to manipulate your system? option A  Trojan option B  Ransomware option C  Worm option D  None of these
Option A Trojan is a type of malware that can look like legitimate software but then performs negative actions to manipulate your system.
Which tool can be used to perform active reconnaissance? option A Nessus option B Nikto option C All of these option D Nmap
Option D Nmap can be used to perform active reconnaissance.

Which of the following is a disadvantage of running legacy platforms and products? option A They do not have modern security features. option B They are often affected by unfixed security vulnerabilities. option C All of these option D When a device is past the last day of support, vendors will not investigate or patch security vulnerabilities in those devices.
Option C All of these are disadvantages of running legacy platforms and products.
